prompt: This program sometimes hangs forever. What is the classic concurrency bug shown here?
choices: Two threads cannot print to System.out simultaneously
t1 acquires lockA and waits for lockB; t2 acquires lockB and waits for lockA — each holds what the other needs, causing deadlock
synchronized blocks cannot be nested in Java
lockA and lockB must be declared volatile to be shared across threads
explanation: Deadlock occurs when two threads each hold a lock the other needs: t1 holds lockA and waits for lockB; t2 holds lockB and waits for lockA. Neither can proceed. Fix: always acquire locks in the same order in all threads (e.g., always lockA then lockB), eliminating the circular wait.
